panels.description: Passed traffic grouped by destination subnet. Helps identify which prefixes are receiving the most traffic.
panels.title: Traffic by Destination Prefix (Packets/s)

panels.description: Same as Packets/s but measured in bits per second. Useful for bandwidth capacity planning.
panels.title: Traffic by Destination Prefix (Bits/s)

panels.description: Top N source autonomous systems by packet count. Identifies which networks are sending the most traffic to your prefixes.
panels.title: Top Source ASNs (Packets/s)

panels.description: Top N source countries by packet count. Shows the geographic distribution of traffic hitting your network.
panels.title: Top Source Countries (Packets/s)

panels.description: Geographic heatmap of traffic volume across Cloudflare colos. Colos are grouped by geohash to de-duplicate co-located facilities.
panels.title: Colo Heatmap

panels.description: Top dropped attacks from dosd, grouped by attack vector and attack ID. Only shows traffic with a non-empty attack ID.
panels.title: Top Attacks (Packets/s)

panels.description: Passed (non-dropped) traffic grouped by IP protocol (TCP, UDP, ICMP, etc.). Shows the protocol mix of legitimate traffic.
panels.title: Passed Traffic by Protocol (Packets/s)

panels.description: Same as Packets/s but measured in bits per second. Useful for seeing which protocols consume the most bandwidth.
panels.title: Passed Traffic by Protocol (Bits/s)

panels.description: Dropped traffic grouped by the mitigation system that blocked it (dosd, flowtrackd, Magic Firewall, etc.).
panels.title: Dropped Traffic Rates By Mitigation System (Packets/s)

panels.description: Same as Packets/s but measured in bits per second. Shows the bandwidth impact of each mitigation system.
panels.title: Dropped Bit Rates By Mitigation System (Bits/s)

panels.description: New TCP connections allowed through flowtrackd, grouped by destination subnet. Shows legitimate connection volume per prefix.
panels.title: New TCP Connections by Destination

panels.description: Packets dropped by dosd grouped by mitigation reason and protocol state. Shows why traffic is being blocked.
panels.title: Drops by Mitigation Reason (Packets/s)

panels.description: SYN cookie challenge events grouped by destination and reason. CHALLENGE_NEEDED = challenge issued, CHALLENGE_PASSED = client completed the handshake.
panels.title: TCP Challenge Activity by Destination (Connections/s)

panels.description: CHALLENGE_NEEDED vs CHALLENGE_PASSED over time. A high ratio of needed-to-passed suggests spoofed SYN floods — spoofed sources cannot complete the SYN cookie handshake.
panels.title: Challenge Success Rate (Connections/s)

panels.description: Top source IPs triggering the most SYN cookie challenges. Useful for identifying repeat offenders or spoofed source addresses.
panels.title: Top Challenged Source IPs

panels.description: Packets dropped by Magic Firewall grouped by rule ID. Identify which firewall rules are triggering most frequently.
panels.title: Drops by Rule ID (Packets/s)

panels.description: Packets dropped by Magic Firewall grouped by destination subnet. Shows which prefixes are targeted by blocked traffic.
panels.title: Drops by Destination (Packets/s)

panels.description: Spectrum traffic grouped by destination IP. Shows packet rate distribution across Spectrum-protected origins.
panels.title: Traffic by Destination IP (Packets/s)

panels.description: Same as Packets/s but measured in bits per second. Shows bandwidth consumption per Spectrum origin.
panels.title: Traffic by Destination IP (Bits/s)

panels.description: Spectrum traffic dropped by destination IP. Shows which origins are receiving the most blocked traffic.
panels.title: Drops by Destination IP (Packets/s)

panels.description: Packets with the IP moreFragments flag set over time. Spikes may indicate fragmentation-based attacks or MTU issues.
panels.title: Fragmented Packet Rate (Packets/s)

panels.description: Same as Packets/s but measured in bits per second. Shows the bandwidth impact of fragmented traffic.
panels.title: Fragmented Traffic Rate (Bits/s)

panels.description: ICMP type 3 code 4 (Destination Unreachable / Fragmentation Needed) packets. These are Path MTU Discovery messages — sustained high rates may indicate MTU black holes.
panels.title: ICMP "Fragmentation Needed" Rate (Packets/s)

panels.description: Top source IPs sending packets with the IP moreFragments flag set. Helps identify sources of fragmented traffic.
panels.title: Top Source IPs Sending Fragments

list.label: Time Bucket
list.name: timeBucket
list.query: 1s : Second,10s : TenSeconds,1m : Minute,5m : FiveMinutes,15m : FifteenMinutes,1h : Hour
